paper_title: A mass-spring model unveils the morphogenesis of phototrophic Diatoma biofilms

methods.description: Construction and setup of a streamside flume to capture the diversity of biofilms from various hydrodynamic environments and growth stages, mimicking a flow landscape typical for streams with low submergence.

steps.description: A streamside flume (length: 40 m) was constructed to capture the diversity of biofilms from various hydrodynamic environments and growth stages.
steps.searchable_summary: construct a 40 m streamside flume for biofilm diversity capture

steps.description: Graded and periodically installed bedforms were installed in the flume, which was continuously fed in a once-through mode with stream water.
steps.searchable_summary: install graded bedforms and feed flume with stream water

steps.description: High-resolution Acoustic Doppler Velocimetry (ADV) was used to capture the three-dimensional flow velocity over the bedforms.
steps.searchable_summary: use ADV to capture 3D flow velocity over bedforms

methods.description: Procedure for growing biofilms on sterile glass slides exposed at different hydrodynamic conditions.

steps.description: Biofilms were grown on initially sterile glass slides that were ignited (450°C, 4 h) to remove organics.
steps.searchable_summary: grow biofilms on sterile glass slides after ignition

steps.description: Replicate glass slides were exposed at the crest and in the trough of repeated bedforms.
steps.searchable_summary: expose glass slides at crest and trough of bedforms

methods.description: Modeling of Diatoma chains as a collection of masses connected by springs to simulate movement, growth, collision, attachment, and sticking processes.

steps.description: Diatoma chains were modeled as an array of particles with mass, connected by three spring types.
steps.searchable_summary: model Diatoma chains as particles connected by springs

steps.description: Chain movement is determined by the motion of each individual particle of that particular chain.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate chain movement based on particle motion

steps.description: The forces acting on each particle were computed and resolved into components in the three directions.
steps.searchable_summary: compute forces on particles and resolve into components

steps.description: Chain growth in length is due to reproduction of Diatoma cells, following an exponential relationship.
steps.searchable_summary: model chain growth based on Diatoma cell reproduction
